dossier.overview:
Resort Docs articles are JSON files — not Markdown pages on the site. Each article has a card in public/data/docs.json and a body in public/docs/articles/{category}/{slug}.json, using the same rich blocks as Research and Features (text, figures, diagrams, code citations, tabs, and more).

Store articles in category folders (meta/, formats/, gameplay/, design/) — never a flat pile in articles/. Longer reference material lives in the pokemon-resort-page repo: docs/AUTHORING.md, docs/TEMPLATE.md, and docs/MEDIA.md.

sections.title: Step-by-step guide
sections.summary: Pick a tab for the task you are working on.

blocks.caption: Start with Getting started if this is your first article.

blocks.body:
Authoring reference files in pokemon-resort-page/ (paths relative to that app root):

• docs/AUTHORING.md — JSON layout, block types, validation, apps, freshness
• docs/TEMPLATE.md — article skeleton, code blocks, write/read tabs
• docs/MEDIA.md — capturing screenshots from the running site
• public/docs/templates/article-template.json — JSON file to copy for a new article

The Docs hub supports search, category and app filters, sort order, and freshness badges. Run npm run docs:freshness (or npm run validate:data) to refresh staleness data.

Run npm run validate:data before publishing changes.

blocks.html:

blocks.title: Suggested reading order
blocks.caption: All paths under pokemon-resort-page/
blocks.source:
flowchart TD
    A[docs/AUTHORING.md] --> B[docs/TEMPLATE.md]
    A --> C[docs/MEDIA.md]
    B --> D[public/docs/templates/article-template.json]
    D --> E[public/docs/articles/category/slug.json]
    E --> F[npm run validate:data]

blocks.body:
See docs/AUTHORING.md for the full checklist. Short version:

1. Pick a slug (lowercase-hyphens) and category, e.g. gameplay / follower-ai-architecture
2. Copy public/docs/templates/article-template.json → public/docs/articles/{category}/{slug}.json
3. Add an articles[] card to public/data/docs.json (unique id + slug + category + apps[] + summary)
4. Replace every REPLACE_* placeholder; delete sections you do not need
5. For guide-style articles, set layout: "tabs-primary" on the main tabs section
6. npm run validate:data from pokemon-resort-page/
7. Optional: edit in Operations Desk → Docs (npm run admin on port 9477)

blocks.caption: Card metadata — one entry per article
blocks.body:
{
  "id": "your-slug",
  "slug": "your-slug",
  "title": "Article title",
  "category": "formats",
  "tags": ["tag1"],
  "apps": ["pokemon-resort", "pokemon-resort-page"],
  "summary": "One line for the docs hub card.",
  "publishedAt": "2026-05-27",
  "updatedAt": "2026-05-27",
  "heroImage": { "path": "media/docs/docs-hub.webp", "caption": "…" },
  "featured": false,
  "author": "Resort Operations"
}

blocks.caption: Article body shape

blocks.body:
Full table in docs/AUTHORING.md. Common blocks:

text — paragraph
figure / image / video — media with caption
diagram — Mermaid UML
code — linked source file (repo + path + body snippet)
tabs — 2+ panels with nested blocks
html — tables (sanitized)
links — https URLs only
compare / carousel / gallery — multi-image layouts

blocks.caption: Example: code block cites a file; tabs split write vs read (see owmap-format article)

blocks.body: repo is the app folder name. path is relative to that folder. Paste the excerpt from the source file into body.

blocks.caption: Minimal code block shape

blocks.body: Nested tabs inside a section — same structure as the tab bar above. Each tab needs id, label, and blocks[].

blocks.caption: Write / Read tab pattern — see docs/TEMPLATE.md

blocks.body:
Use captures from the running site — not mockups or placeholder art.

From pokemon-resort-page/:

npm run dev          → 127.0.0.1:5174
npm run admin        → 127.0.0.1:9477
npm run docs:screenshots

Writes public/media/docs/*.webp. Reference in JSON as media/docs/filename.webp.

Full guide: docs/MEDIA.md
Captured with npm run docs:screenshots while the Vite dev server was running.
blocks.caption: Docs hub — browser screenshot

blocks.body:
Copy public/docs/templates/article-template.json to public/docs/articles/{category}/{slug}.json.

Category folders:
• meta/ — authoring guides
• formats/ — file format specs
• gameplay/ — systems & architecture
• design/ — visual / UX reference

Default sections in the template:
• summary — intro
• concepts — definitions + optional diagram
• implementation — code blocks + write/read tabs
• reference — HTML tables
• media — screenshot placeholder
• related — cross-app notes

Preview on site: #/docs?article=article-template
Details: docs/TEMPLATE.md

blocks.title: Publish path
blocks.caption: After validate:data passes
blocks.source:
flowchart LR
    A[Copy template JSON] --> B[Edit docs.json card]
    B --> C[validate:data]
    C --> D[Git commit]
    D --> E[GitHub Pages]
    E --> F[#/docs hub]
